author: alert-bot
text: 🔴 ALERT: Pipeline healthcheck FAILED. Analytics API returning HTTP 503. Service: analytics-api. Severity: P1.

author: marcus.johnson
text: ACK. I'm looking into it. Checking the analytics-api logs now.

author: marcus.johnson
text: Found it. The cache layer is serving stale data. The analytics pipeline detects the inconsistency with the source of truth and crashes as a safety measure. Looking at why the cache isn't invalidating properly.

author: james.martinez
text: I deployed v3.2.1 at 13:45 UTC — the real-time dashboard feature. Could my deploy have caused this? Should I rollback?

author: marcus.johnson
text: Don't rollback yet, James. The cache issue isn't in v3.2.1 itself. It's a pre-existing race condition in the cache invalidation logic — it's been latent since we introduced the cache layer in v2.8. Your deploy increased write frequency by ~3x, which finally triggered the race condition. The bug was always there.

author: tom.anderson
text: I've got a cache TTL hotfix ready — reducing TTL from 300s to 30s to limit the stale data window. Deploying to canary now. This is a temporary fix, not a permanent solution.

author: tom.anderson
text: Canary looks stable. Cache miss rate is up ~40% as expected, but no stale data issues. Rolling to full production.

author: alert-bot
text: 📊 Analytics API: Brief recovery detected 14:46:32 to 14:46:58 UTC (26 seconds) before failing again. Likely a transient cache refresh.

author: marcus.johnson
text: Fix confirmed across all pipeline stages. Error rate back to baseline (0.02%). All analytics endpoints responding normally.

author: marcus.johnson
text: Marking incident INC-2026-017 as RESOLVED. Total downtime: approximately 2 hours 24 minutes (14:23 to 16:47 UTC). Root cause: cache invalidation race condition. Temporary fix: reduced cache TTL. Permanent fix needed: distributed lock (task_602).

author: lisa.chen
text: We need to have a serious conversation about deployment practices. This outage happened because we shipped a feature that tripled write volume without any load testing. We can't keep doing this.

author: marcus.johnson
text: To be clear — the deploy didn't cause this outage. The race condition has been latent since v2.8. v3.2.1 increased write frequency which triggered it, but any feature that increased cache writes would have done the same. The real issue is we never implemented the distributed lock we promised after the November incident.

author: james.martinez
text: Thanks for the clarity, Marcus. I was worried I broke production. For what it's worth, the staging tests all passed — the race condition doesn't reproduce at staging-level write volumes.

author: sarah.kim
text: We need a formal post-incident review. @alex.chen can you own the PIR? Let's present at 3pm tomorrow. Make sure to include the timeline, root cause, customer impact, and action items with owners.

author: tom.anderson
text: Important note: the cache TTL hotfix is TEMPORARY. It works but cache miss rate is up 40%, which will impact p99 latency under load. We need the proper distributed lock (task_602) implemented ASAP. I've also filed task_603 for SLA monitoring.
